name: graphql-core (Python GraphQL Reference Implementation)

auth.notes: Low-level parsing/execution library; no auth concept

performance.notes: Pure Python schema parsing and execution; overhead is minimal

agent_readiness.notes: Python port of graphql-js reference implementation. from graphql import build_schema, graphql_sync, graphql. schema = build_schema('type Query { hello: String }'). root = {'hello': lambda obj, info: 'world'}. result = graphql_sync(schema, '{ hello }', root_value=root). Async: result = await graphql(schema, query). from graphql import parse, validate, execute. ast = parse('{ hello }'). errors = validate(schema, ast). result = execute(schema, ast, root_value=root). SDL: build_schema() from SDL string. Type builder: GraphQLSchema(query=GraphQLObjectType('Query', {...})). Used internally by Graphene, Strawberry, Ariadne.

gotchas: Very low-level — most users should use Graphene, Strawberry, or Ariadne instead
GraphQL query depth/complexity limits must be implemented manually
No HTTP server included — integrate with ASGI/WSGI framework yourself
Type-annotated but complex type definitions require GraphQL type system knowledge
Subscriptions require async generators — non-trivial to implement
best_when: Building GraphQL framework integrations or tooling
Schema parsing and validation without full framework overhead
When Graphene/Strawberry internals need to be customized
avoid_when: Building GraphQL APIs for production use — use Graphene or Strawberry
When SDL-first development is preferred (use Ariadne)
summary: graphql-core is the Python port of graphql-js reference implementation. build_schema(SDL), parse(), validate(), graphql_sync(), execute(). Async support. Type-annotated. MIT by Christoph Zwerschke. Used internally by Graphene, Strawberry, and Ariadne. Best for GraphQL tooling and schema validation — most developers should use Graphene or Strawberry instead. Very low-level. No HTTP server. Depth limits manual.
